objectives: Explain core concepts and components of database management systems
Perform essential tasks to operate relational database management system
Identify common administrative responsibilities across major RDBMS platforms
learning_path.title: Foundations of Database Management Systems and RDBMS Operations

objectives: Plan and execute database installation and configuration steps
Validate baseline configuration for reliability and maintainability
Compare configuration considerations for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server
learning_path.title: Database Installation and Configuration Across Platforms

objectives: Design relational schemas aligned to application requirements
Define tables, relationships, and constraints to support data integrity
Evaluate schema tradeoffs for performance and maintainability
learning_path.title: Data Modeling and Schema Design

objectives: Explain transaction boundaries and isolation behavior
Identify and mitigate common concurrency anomalies
Apply concurrency control concepts to operational troubleshooting
learning_path.title: Transaction Management and Concurrency Control

objectives: Select appropriate indexing strategies for common access patterns
Diagnose inefficient queries and improve execution behavior
Validate performance impact of indexing changes
learning_path.title: Indexing and Query Optimization

objectives: Establish performance baselines and identify bottlenecks
Tune database settings and workload patterns for improved throughput and latency
Prioritize tuning actions based on measurable impact
learning_path.title: Database Performance Tuning

objectives: Define key health and performance indicators for databases
Set up monitoring and alerting strategies to detect issues early
Use monitoring signals to guide troubleshooting and tuning
learning_path.title: Database Monitoring and Alerting

objectives: Implement access control aligned to least-privilege principles
Manage authentication and authorization for database users and roles
Assess and reduce common security risks in database environments
learning_path.title: Database Security and Access Control

objectives: Design backup strategies to meet recovery objectives
Execute restore and recovery workflows and validate outcomes
Document and test recovery procedures for operational readiness
learning_path.title: Backup and Recovery Planning and Execution

objectives: Explain replication and clustering concepts and common architectures
Select replication and clustering approaches based on workload needs
Identify operational considerations and failure modes in replicated systems
learning_path.title: Replication and Clustering for Scale and Resilience

objectives: Design HADR strategies aligned to availability and recovery requirements
Plan failover and failback procedures and validate readiness
Integrate monitoring, backups, and replication into an HADR runbook
learning_path.title: High Availability and Disaster Recovery (HADR) Design and Operations

matched_occupation.description: Database administrators test, implement and administer computer databases. They use their expertise in database management systems to plan, coordinate and implement security measures to safeguard computer databases. They also use scripts and configuration files to tailor a database to users' needs.
matched_occupation.label: database administrator
